title: Accelerating Quantum Computing with AI and Robotics: A Self-Verifying Dossier
description: How AI and robotics might accelerate fault-tolerant quantum computing, argued by descending from surface-level engineering wins to engineered dissipation at the floor. Includes a reproducible cat-qubit simulation, a Knill-Laflamme proof that the four-component cat makes single-photon loss a correctable Z4 syndrome, and an honest claim ledger. No novel physics is claimed; the contribution is a reproducible, self-verifying synthesis. An Open Dossier publication with executable claims under CI and published adversarial review.
